item.title: They Grew Up With Smartphones. This Is How They Live Without Them.
item.description: We asked six young New Yorkers who have taken the leap.

item.title: Is There Life After Smartphones?
item.description: What happens when a growing cohort of young people decide to ditch their devices?

item.title: My Friend Wrote a Book About Her Trauma. Do I Have to Read It?
item.description: She told me that anyone who won’t read her book is not her friend and that I am contributing to the greater problem in the world.

item.title: The Unlikely TV Show Restoring Everyone’s Faith in Dating
item.description: Without exploitation, “Love on the Spectrum” captures the triumphs and travails of dating. It has become one of Netflix’s most popular shows.

item.title: Worried About A.I. Taking Your Job? That’s Not Very ‘Agentic’ of You.
item.description: Today’s spin on the idea of personal agency is convenient for tech C.E.O.s, who boast that their models work just fine without us.

item.title: Ben Lerner’s Latest Is a Strange and Brilliant Attempt to Resurrect the Novel
item.description: With “Transcription,” the writer makes a case for the vitality of the form.

item.title: Is There a Proper Way to Eat Chip Dust?
item.description: A ruling on the consumption of snack dregs.

item.title: America Is Used to Hiding Its Wars. Trump Is Doing the Opposite.
item.description: The administration’s videos are possible only in a country that asks little from its people beyond their YouTube clicks.

item.title: My Fiancé’s Mother Is Out of Control. Does She Have to Come to the Wedding?
item.description: I can’t put my family through what she put us through on Thanksgiving.

item.title: Forks in the Dishwasher — Tines Up or Down?
item.description: A ruling on a timeless marital dispute.

item.title: How Did ‘Gatekeeping’ Become a Cardinal Sin?
item.description: We decided that filtering information was malicious. That didn’t mean we stopped doing it.

item.title: My Brother Keeps Falling for Online Romance Scams. Should I Bail Him Out Again?
item.description: When I hesitate to give him money, he guilts me.

item.title: The Next Phase of the Immigration Crackdown Is Quieter — and More Destabilizing
item.description: Self-deportation is an idea with deep roots. It’s making a comeback.
